Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Business Corporations Act (purpose of a corporation)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Business Corporations Act (purpose of a corporation)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Business Corporations Act to provide that a corporationâs purpose is to pursue its best interests while also operating in a manner that

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: benefits the wider society and the environment in a manner proportionate to its size and the nature of its operations; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: minimizes any harm that the corporation causes to the wider society and the environment, with the objective of eliminating such harm.
Whereas Parliament recognizes that a review of the purpose of a corporation is required as a result of the social and environmental challenges of the 21st century;
Whereas corporations should focus on producing profitable solutions for the problems of people and the planet and not on profiting from creating problems;

Whereas directors and officers have historically had a fiduciary duty to act only in the best interests of their corporations and this has resulted in social and environmental factors being regularly considered as either risks or opportunities for those corporationsâ operations and profitability, not in relation to those corporationsâ impacts on broader society and the environment;
Whereas Parliament considers it essential for corporations to have a purpose that encompasses both the pursuit of their best interests and benefits to the wider society and the environment; and
Whereas Parliament considers it essential that corporations periodically disclose information to the public about these impacts and benefits;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the 21st-Century Business Act.
The Canada Business Corporations Act is amended by adding the following after subsection 6(1):

Subsection.Text: The articles may set out the purpose of the corporation, provided that the purpose is consistent with section 14.1.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 14:
The purpose of a corporation is to pursue its best interests while also operating in a manner that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: benefits the wider society and the environment in a manner proportionate to its size and the nature of its operations; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: minimizes any harm that the corporation causes to the wider society and the environment, with the objective of eliminating such harm.

Section.Text: Subsections 122(1) and (1.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Directors and officers â duty to pursue purpose of corporation

Subsection.Text: Every director and officer of a corporation, in exercising their powers and discharging their duties, shall act honestly and in good faith

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with a view to the best interests of the corporation; and

Text.Ins: to pursue those interests while ensuring that the corporation operates in a manner that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: benefits the wider society and the environment in a manner proportionate to its size and the nature of its operations, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: minimizes any harm that it causes to the wider society and the environment, with the objective of eliminating such harm.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Directors and officers â duty of care
Subsection.Text: Every director and officer of a corporation, in exercising their powers and discharging their duties, shall exercise the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would exercise in comparable circumstances, including towards

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shareholders;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: employees;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: retirees and pensioners;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: creditors;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: consumers;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: governments;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the environment; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the corporationâs long-term interests.

Subsection.Text: Every director and officer of a corporation in exercising their powers and discharging their duties shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: act honestly and in good faith with a view to the best interests of the corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: exercise the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would exercise in comparable circumstances.

Subsection.Text: When acting with a view to the best interests of the corporation under paragraph (1)(a), the directors and officers of the corporation may consider, but are not limited to, the following factors:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the interests of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the environment; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the long-term interests of the corporation.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 123(5) before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director has complied with his or her duties under paragraph 122(1)(a) and subsection 122(1.1) if the director relied in good faith on

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 5:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant poriton of subsection 123(5):

Subsection.Text: A director has complied with his or her duties under subsection 122(1) if the director relied in good faith on

Section.Text: Paragraph 124(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acted honestly and in good faith to advance the purpose of the corporation or, as the case may be, to advance the purpose of the other entity for which the individual acted as a director or officer or in a similar such capacity at the corporationâs request; and

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 6:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant portions of subsection 124(3):

Subsection.Text: A corporation may not indemnify an individual under subsection (1) unless the individual

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acted honestly and in good faith with a view to the best interests of the corporation, or, as the case may be, to the best interests of the other entity for which the individual acted as director or officer or in a similar capacity at the corporationâs request; and

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 172.1:

Heading.Label: PART XIV.2
Heading.TitleText: Disclosure Relating to Impacts on the Wider Society and the Environment

Subsection.Text: At every annual meeting of its shareholders, the corporation shall report in the prescribed manner on its impacts on the wider society and the environment, including on the measures it has taken to minimize any harm it causes to the wider society and the environment.
A corporation may comply with subsection (1) either by providing a report in respect of the corporation or by being party to a joint report in respect of affiliated corporations.
Regulations prescribing how the report referred to in subsection (1) shall be made may require the corporation or affiliated corporations to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: select a prescribed third-party standard for assessing the corporationâs or affiliated corporationsâ impacts on the wider society and the environment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: assess the corporationâs or affiliated corporationsâ impacts on the wider society and the environment against that standard for the most recently completed financial year.

Subsection.Text: Regulations prescribing how the report referred to in subsection (1) shall be made may prescribe different manners that are based on the corporationâs size.

The corporation shall provide the report referred to in subsection (1) to each shareholder â except to a shareholder who has informed the corporation in writing that they do not want to receive that information â by sending the information with the notice referred to in subsection 135(1) or by making the information available along with a proxy circular referred to in subsection 150(1).

The corporation shall concurrently send the report referred to in subsection (1) to the Director and make it available to the public, including by publishing the report in a prominent place on the corporationâs website.
Paragraph 239(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it appears to be consistent with the purpose of the corporation or its subsidiary that the action be brought, prosecuted, defended or discontinued.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 8: (1)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant portions of subsection 239(2):

Subsection.Text: No action may be brought and no intervention in an action may be made under subsection (1) unless the court is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it appears to be in the interests of the corporation or its subsidiary that the action be brought, prosecuted, defended or discontinued.

Subsection.Text: Section 239 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

A person who makes an application under subsection (1) in relation to the duties of a director or officer under paragraph 122(1)(b) is deemed to be a proper person under paragraph (d) of the definition of complainant in section 238 if the court is satisfied that the person is acting in pursuit of the interests of the wider society or the environment.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on the first anniversary of the day on which it receives royal assent.
